title: Laurentia bivonae
taxonomy: Laurentia bivonae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Laurentia, which is a group of plants native to the Mediterranean region. Laurentia bivonae is a perennial herb with blue or white flowers and is commonly found in rocky areas and mountainous regions. The plant is often used as an ornamental in gardens due to its attractive flowers and low maintenance requirements. Laurentia bivonae is also known as the "Alpine Forget-me-not" due to its similarity in appearance to the forget-me-not flower.

intro: Laurentia bivonae, also known as the Bluebell of Madeira, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Campanulaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections, such as bronchitis and asthma.
